heading: “Plain language” requirement for Federal banking agency rules
label: 4809 “Plain language” requirement for Federal banking agency rules
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, and not as part of title III of which comprises this chapter.Pub. L. 103–322

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Each Federal banking agency shall use plain language in all proposed and final rulemakings published by the agency in the Federal Register after .January 1, 2000

children.heading: Report
children.label: b Report
children.text: Not later than , each Federal banking agency shall submit to the Congress a report that describes how the agency has complied with subsection (a).March 1, 2001

children.heading: Definition
children.label: c Definition
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “Federal banking agency” has the meaning given that term in .section 1813 of this title
